prediction.reasoning: (1) MATCH CHARACTER — This is a DEFENSIVE_BATTLE. The cascading profiles show STRONG convergence (64%) for Under 2.5 Goals, with Leganes's home defensive record (0.40 GA/m last five, 60% clean sheet rate) clashing with Eibar's away attack (0.25 GF/m last five, 0% Over 2.5 rate). The Tactician's analysis confirms a 'tight, low-event match' with both teams deploying structured, defensively-oriented formations. (2) DIRECTION EVIDENCE — The mathematical evidence is overwhelming. The Quant's Poisson model shows a 15.7pp edge on Under 2.5 Goals (64% vs 48.3% market implied). The EdgeCalculator identifies Under 1.75 Goals with a 35.2% edge — the highest of any market — supported by an 79.3% internal probability. The λ sum (home + away goal expectancy) is exceptionally low, anchored by Eibar's 0.25 away goals per match. (3) AGENTS CONSENSUS — The direction is strongly aligned: The Quant flags Under 2.5 as a top value bet; The Tactician predicts 'fewer goals than average'; The Archivist notes a historical average of 1.7 goals and two consecutive 0-0 draws; The Auditor recommends Under 1.75 as the strongest value opportunity; The Sceptic's 'surprise scenario' is a narrow 1-0. The only dissent is the Away Scout's flawed 'rising' form assessment, which The Sceptic critically challenged. (4) RISKS — The primary risk is a single early goal altering the match dynamic, though both teams' profiles suggest they lack the attacking potency to force a high-scoring game. The unknown injury status of key players introduces minor execution risk, but squad depth figures suggest adequate cover. The psychological impact of Leganes's recently suspended match remains an unquantified variable.

script.title: Leganes vs Eibar: The Fortress vs The Road Ghosts
script.hook: What happens when an impenetrable home defence meets a team that cannot score on the road? Leganes have conceded just two goals in their last five home matches. Eibar have failed to win in thirteen away attempts this season. This is a statistical collision that should create betting value — but the bookmakers have already priced it perfectly.
script.chapter_home: Leganes arrive with a paradox. Their home form shows remarkable defensive resilience — just 0.40 goals conceded per match over their last five at the Estadio Municipal de Butarque, with clean sheets in 60% of those games. Yet their overall results lack conviction, with just one win in their last five matches across all venues. Manager Asier Garitano typically deploys a compact 4-4-2 that prioritises defensive structure over attacking flair. Their squad depth of 34 registered players suggests adequate cover, though the psychological impact of their recently suspended match against Sporting Gijon remains an unquantified variable. The narrative here is one of a fortress built on sand — solid at the back but struggling to translate that security into three points.
script.chapter_away: Eibar present a tale of two forms. Their overall momentum appears positive with three wins in their last five matches, but this masks a catastrophic away record. They are yet to win on the road this season — zero victories from thirteen attempts — and their attack has been virtually non-existent, scoring just 0.25 goals per game in their last five away matches. Manager Joseba Etxeberria favours a 4-2-3-1 setup that becomes cautious and draw-prone when travelling. While their defensive numbers away are respectable at 0.75 goals conceded per match, their chronic inability to score creates a fundamental problem. The central tension is whether their improved home momentum can finally breach a fortress they have consistently failed to conquer.
script.chapter_history: The historical pattern between these sides reinforces the low-scoring narrative. Across ten meetings, there is no dominant side — Leganes have won 30%, Eibar just 20%, with a significant 40% ending level. The average goals per match stands at just 1.7, with only 30% of games seeing over 2.5 goals. Most tellingly, the last two meetings have finished 0-0 — consecutive stalemates that highlight the cagey, risk-averse nature of this fixture. This history creates a psychological backdrop where both sides may approach the match tentatively, fearing the first mistake that could break the pattern.
script.chapter_analysis: The tactical matchup reveals why goals should be scarce. Leganes's compact 4-4-2 provides a solid defensive block that matches up well against Eibar's 4-2-3-1. The home side will likely dominate possession, but their slow build-up and lack of attacking potency play directly into Eibar's preferred away strategy of sitting in a mid-block and looking to counter. The Sceptic critically challenged the Away Scout's assessment of Eibar's 'rising' form, highlighting how their overall results mask a historically awful and currently stagnant away attack. This challenge was validated by the cascading data showing Eibar's away momentum as 'stable' at a very poor level. The Tactician's analysis predicts a 'tight, low-event match' with both teams deploying structured, defensively-oriented formations. The mathematical evidence is overwhelming — the Quant's Poisson model shows a 15.7 percentage point edge on Under 2.5 Goals, while the EdgeCalculator identifies Under 1.75 Goals with a 35.2% edge, the highest of any market.
script.chapter_value: Our market: NO BET. The Boot Room's analysis confirms a strong directional consensus toward a low-scoring match, with 64% profile convergence for Under 2.5 Goals and an internal probability of 79.3% for Under 1.75 Goals. However, the bookmakers have priced this reality into the market with remarkable efficiency. While the mathematical edge exists on paper, no available line meets our strict qualifying criteria of conviction equalling strong with odds at 1.70 or greater. The value has been extracted before we could reach it. Sometimes the smartest play is recognising when the market has already adjusted.
script.ending: The Boot Room's verdict is clear: pass on this fixture. The analysis points overwhelmingly toward a low-scoring stalemate between Leganes's home defensive fortress and Eibar's chronic away-day impotence. Yet the bookmakers have priced this narrative perfectly, leaving no qualifying value opportunity. Confidence in any market recommendation stands at 0%. In betting, discipline means knowing when to walk away. Boot Room out.
script.roundup_summary:
Leganes versus Eibar. Sixteenth hosts eleventh in the Segunda División, with just four points separating the sides. Leganes arrive with poor overall form — just one win in their last five — but an excellent home defensive record of 0.40 goals conceded per match over their last five at Butarque. Eibar show positive momentum with three wins in five, but this masks a catastrophic away record: zero wins in thirteen attempts, scoring just 0.25 goals per game in their last five road trips.

The historical head-to-head record reveals a cagey, low-scoring fixture. Across ten meetings, the average goals per match is just 1.7, with a significant 40% ending level. The last two encounters finished 0-0, extending a pattern of stalemates. This season, Leganes have failed to score in 38% of home matches, while Eibar have failed to score in seven of thirteen away games.

The Boot Room's analysis identified overwhelming evidence for a low-scoring match. The Poisson model showed a 15.7 percentage point edge on Under 2.5 Goals, with team profile convergence at 64%. The EdgeCalculator identified Under 1.75 Goals with a 35.2% edge — the highest of any market — supported by an internal probability of 79.3%. The λ sum of home and away goal expectancy was exceptionally low, anchored by Eibar's 0.25 away goals per match. The Sceptic critically challenged the Away Scout's 'rising' form assessment, highlighting how it masked Eibar's historically awful away attack. The bookmakers have significantly underpriced the likelihood of a low-scoring match relative to the mathematical evidence, but no qualifying line met our strict criteria.

The Boot Room market is NO BET. While the directional analysis strongly favours under goals, no available market offers sufficient mathematical edge to qualify for a recommendation. The bookmakers have priced the low-scoring narrative efficiently, leaving no value opportunity that meets our conviction and odds thresholds. Boot Room market: NO BET. Confidence: 0%.
